name: Lager / Pilsner
cultural_notes: The most popular beer style worldwide. Pilsner originated in Plzeň, Czechia in 1842.
safety_notes: Low ABV makes it easier to manage consumption
Standard hydration rules apply
recommendations: Ideal for long social sessions
Alternate with water if drinking in hot climates

name: IPA
cultural_notes: Originally brewed with extra hops to preserve beer during long sea voyages to India.
safety_notes: Higher alcohol content than standard lagers
Can lead to faster intoxication than expected
recommendations: Limit consumption to 1-2 per hour
Eat a meal beforehand due to higher acidity/hops

name: Stout / Porter
cultural_notes: Stout evolved from porter. Guinness from Ireland is the world's most famous stout.
safety_notes: Filling nature often naturally slows down drinking pace
Standard beer precautions
recommendations: Enjoy slowly to appreciate flavor complexity
Pairs well with heavy food

name: Light Lager
cultural_notes: Developed in the 1970s as a lower-calorie alternative to regular lagers.
safety_notes: Lowest risk for intoxication per unit
High water content
risk_factors: Volume-based intoxication (drinking too many because they feel 'light')
recommendations: Best for high-heat environments
Good choice for maintaining awareness in social settings

name: Craft IPA
cultural_notes: Part of the American craft beer revolution that began in the 1980s.
safety_notes: Significant ABV (often 7%+) puts this closer to wine in terms of impact
Hops can cause digestive sensitivity in some
risk_factors: Stealth intoxication due to high flavor masking
recommendations: Always check the ABV on the tap list
Treat one pint as roughly 1.5 - 2 standard drinks

name: Pale Ale
cultural_notes: The original pale ales were developed in England using coke-fired malt.
safety_notes: Standard session beer risk profile
Predictable ABV range
recommendations: A safe middle-ground for most social occasions

name: Pilsner (South America)
cultural_notes: South American pilsners are often lighter and more refreshing than their European counterparts.
safety_notes: Safe for hot climates
Watch for electrolyte loss if drinking in sun
recommendations: Drink alongside plenty of water in tropical heat

name: Adjunct Lager
cultural_notes: Uses adjuncts like rice or corn to lighten body and flavor. Includes major global brands.
safety_notes: Predictable and standard impact
Consistent production standards
risk_factors: Fast consumption due to light body
recommendations: Standard hydration applies

name: Traditional Lagers (Europe)
cultural_notes: Includes German Helles, Czech Světlý ležák, and Vienna Lager.
safety_notes: Consistent quality and ABV
Often served in larger volumes (Steins)
recommendations: Eat traditional 'pub food' (pretzels, meats) to buffer alcohol

name: Trappist Ales
cultural_notes: Brewed by Trappist monks. Only 14 monasteries worldwide are authorized to use the 'Authentic Trappist Product' logo.
safety_notes: Very high ABV (7-11%) disguised by complex sweetness
Secondary fermentation in the bottle increases carbonation speed
risk_factors: Strong flavor hides high alcohol presence
recommendations: Treat as a sipping drink, similar to wine
Limit to one per session if maintaining high alertness

name: Roggenbier
cultural_notes: A rye beer from Germany, similar to Hefeweizen but made with rye malt. Nearly extinct before craft revival.
safety_notes: Standard alcohol risk profile
Hearty body often prevents rapid drinking
risk_factors: High grain particulate content may cause digestive sensitivity
recommendations: Excellent pairing with heavy German cuisine
Standard hydration applies

name: Joppenbier
cultural_notes: A historical strong beer from Thuringia, Germany, revived by craft brewers.
safety_notes: High residual sugar content can lead to worse hangovers
High ABV for a traditional style
recommendations: Drink alongside high-protein food
Double your water intake due to sugar content

name: Sorghum Beer (Umqombothi)
cultural_notes: A traditional South African beer made from sorghum and maize. Thick and rich in B vitamins.
safety_notes: Unfiltered texture is heavy on the stomach
Low alcohol but consumed in large communal volumes
risk_factors: Short shelf life; risk of spoilage if not fresh
recommendations: Consume within 48 hours of brewing
Ensure source is reputable

name: Millet Beer (Bil Bil, Pito)
cultural_notes: Known as Bil Bil in Cameroon and Pito in Ghana. Central to social life.
safety_notes: Low ABV makes it relatively safe for long gatherings
Naturally rich in nutrients
risk_factors: High acidity can be harsh on an empty stomach
recommendations: Drink in social groups to gauge local consumption norms
Pair with light snacks

name: Burukutu
cultural_notes: A traditional beer from northern Nigeria and Ghana made from sorghum or millet.
safety_notes: Standard traditional beer risk profile
risk_factors: Active fermentation can lead to bloating
recommendations: Standard drinking precautions apply
Best consumed when freshly made

name: Lagers (Africa)
cultural_notes: Major commercial breweries produce icons like Tusker and Star.
safety_notes: Consistent industrial standards
Safe for general consumption
recommendations: A reliable and safe 'default' drink in the region

name: Mid-strength Beer (Australia)
cultural_notes: Popular for session drinking in hot weather to stay safe and hydrated.
safety_notes: Lower ABV significantly reduces the rate of impairment
High drinkability
risk_factors: Low ABV can lead to over-confidence in driving (check local laws)
recommendations: Ideal for maintaining a consistent buzz without losing alertness

name: Zero-alcohol Beer
cultural_notes: Growing trend as health consciousness rises. Technology has improved flavor.
safety_notes: Safe for nearly all users, including those on many medications
No motor skill impairment
risk_factors: Trace alcohol (<0.5%) can be relevant for specific clinical conditions
recommendations: Use to 'bridge' the gap between alcoholic drinks to slow total consumption

name: Murree Lager (Pakistan)
cultural_notes: Pakistan's oldest brewery. Primarily for non-Muslims and export.
safety_notes: Major risks are legal/regulatory rather than chemical
Standard lager alcohol impact
recommendations: Only consume in legally designated areas
Keep all purchase permits on your person
legal_notes: Only available to non-Muslims with permits in Pakistan
